meta.title: Computational Science Engineering — RLGC Tracker
meta.description: Standing program tracker for re-engineering every science and mathematical sub-discipline for CI+human teams. Each domain gets four operations: REDUCE (strip to AC(0) core), LINEARIZE (convert to linear algebra where possible), GRAPH (wire into AC theorem graph), CONNECT (bridge to other domains). Updated daily as part of the living library discipline.
meta.prototype: BST/AC — the theorem graph IS the first science engineered this way

grading.reduce: DONE / PARTIAL / NOT_STARTED — has the AC(0) core been identified?
grading.linearize: DONE / PARTIAL / NOT_STARTED — is the domain expressed in linear algebra?
grading.graph: WIRED / PARTIAL / SEEDED / EMPTY — how connected is the domain in the AC graph?
grading.connect: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — how many cross-domain bridges exist?
grading.overall: A (fully engineered) / B (mostly done) / C (partial) / D (seeded only) / F (untouched) / P (planned, not yet started)
